profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; tends to lead meeting logistics and wants a clear retirement timeline to age 68.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with account types and performance talk, asks about taxes and trade-offs, but prefers the advisor to translate strategy into a simple plan.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but protective: willing to take some market risk if the plan shows downside limits; gets uneasy when outcomes feel open-ended and will ask 'what’s the worst-case year?'
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; uses numbers and specific references, occasionally corrects details. Speaks in complete thoughts, but will hedge with 'roughly' or 'if I’m remembering right' when it’s not his account. Uses light filler like 'so' and 'okay' when processing.
emotional_tendencies: anxious about market drawdowns near retirement
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and comparative: wants 2–3 options with pros/cons, then decides after a brief recap; prefers to sleep on big changes but will commit if the rationale is tight.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to recommendations, but expects transparency on fees, taxes, and why a product or allocation is being suggested.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market headlines influence his comfort level)
hidden_concerns: worries they could retire and then face a bad sequence of returns
concerned about paying more tax than necessary and missing planning opportunities

profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; manages day-to-day cash flow and benefits paperwork, and brings up health-related considerations and stability needs; targets retirement around age 70.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands budgeting and the basics of retirement accounts, but mixes up terms (gross vs net, balance vs contribution) and prefers plain-English explanations over charts.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More conservative in day-to-day behavior: prefers predictable income and cash buffers; will agree to moderate risk if she hears 'capital preservation' and a clear plan for withdrawals and emergencies.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers and stories ('it feels like…'), asks clarifying questions, and sometimes interrupts to confirm she understood. Uses hedges like 'I think' and 'maybe' and admits when she doesn’t remember exact figures.
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations get too technical
sensitive to uncertainty around health and work stamina
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven: wants David and the advisor aligned; prefers a short summary and 'what do we do next' checklist before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established: values responsiveness and patience; trust increases when the advisor repeats back her concerns accurately.
internal_biases: ambiguity aversion (dislikes plans with too many moving parts)
hidden_concerns: worries about maintaining lifestyle if one of them needs to stop working earlier than planned
concerned about having enough liquid cash on hand even if investments look strong
